pattern: Within each maximal run of same-colored balloons, keep only the priciest one to remove and pay the rest.
hints: Only consecutive equal-colored neighbors are a problem, so think in terms of maximal runs of the same letter.
Inside a run you must remove every balloon but one, and removing costs the balloon's time, so removing all-but-one always pays the sum of the run minus whichever one you spare.
To minimize cost, spare the single most expensive balloon in each run; sum (run total - run max) over every run of length >= 2.

edge_inputs.description: no two adjacent balloons share a color
one long run of the same color
the most expensive balloon in a run sits in the middle
several separate runs of different colors with distinct costs
alternating colors with an occasional accidental repeat

stress.description: long random string over a small alphabet with random costs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    alphabet = 'abcde'
    colors = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    times = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [colors, times]
stress.note: small alphabet forces frequent runs of length >= 2

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minCost(self, colors, neededTime):
        n = len(colors)
        total = 0
        i = 0
        while i < n:
            j = i
            group_sum = 0
            group_max = 0
            while j < n and colors[j] == colors[i]:
                group_sum += neededTime[j]
                if neededTime[j] > group_max:
                    group_max = neededTime[j]
                j += 1
            total += group_sum - group_max
            i = j
        return total
